questions.text: Who is called the king of the jungle?
questions.explanation: The lion is called the king of the jungle.

questions.text: Which bird is the national bird of India?
questions.explanation: The peacock is the national bird of India.

questions.text: A ____ is the largest animal on land.
questions.explanation: The elephant is the largest land animal.

questions.text: A parrot can talk by copying sounds.
questions.explanation: Parrots can copy sounds and 'talk'. True.

questions.text: What is the national flower of India?
questions.explanation: The lotus is the national flower of India.

questions.text: What is the national animal of India?
questions.explanation: The Royal Bengal Tiger is the national animal of India.

questions.text: Our national flag has ____ colours.
questions.explanation: The Indian flag is tricolour — saffron, white and green (three colours).

questions.text: The national fruit of India is the mango.
questions.explanation: Mango is the national fruit of India. True.

questions.text: What colour do you get by mixing nothing — the colour of the sky on a clear day?
questions.explanation: The clear daytime sky is blue.

questions.text: A shape with three sides is a ____.
questions.explanation: A triangle has three sides.

questions.text: Grass is usually ____ in colour.
questions.explanation: Grass is green.

questions.text: A banana is yellow when ripe.
questions.explanation: A ripe banana is yellow. True.

questions.text: Which one is a vegetable?
questions.explanation: Potato is a vegetable; the others are fruits.

questions.text: Which fruit is red and round and keeps the doctor away?
questions.explanation: 'An apple a day keeps the doctor away.' Apple is red and round.

questions.text: Carrot is orange and is a ____.
questions.explanation: Carrot is a vegetable.

questions.text: Orange is a fruit.
questions.explanation: Orange is a juicy fruit. True.

questions.text: How many days are there in a week?
questions.explanation: There are 7 days in a week.

questions.text: Which day comes after Sunday?
questions.explanation: After Sunday comes Monday.

questions.text: There are ____ months in a year.
questions.explanation: A year has 12 months.

questions.text: Sunday is a holiday for most schools.
questions.explanation: Most schools have a holiday on Sunday. True.

questions.text: What is the capital of India?
questions.explanation: New Delhi is the capital of India.

questions.text: Which is our national language written in Devanagari script?
questions.explanation: Hindi, written in Devanagari, is an official language of India.

questions.text: We live in the country called ____.
questions.explanation: We live in India.

questions.text: The Indian flag has a wheel called the Ashok Chakra in the middle.
questions.explanation: The Ashok Chakra (blue wheel) is in the centre of the flag. True.

questions.text: Which star gives us light in the day?
questions.explanation: The Sun is a star that gives us light during the day.

questions.text: We see the moon mostly at ____.
questions.explanation: The moon is mostly seen at night.

questions.text: Ice is frozen ____.
questions.explanation: Ice is water that has frozen into a solid.

questions.text: The Sun rises in the east.
questions.explanation: The Sun rises in the east and sets in the west. True.

questions.text: Before crossing the road we should look ____.
questions.explanation: Always look both sides before crossing the road.

questions.text: Which traffic light colour means STOP?
questions.explanation: Red light means stop.

questions.text: Green light means ____.
questions.explanation: Green light means go.

questions.text: We should say 'please' and 'thank you'.
questions.explanation: Saying please and thank you is good manners. True.

questions.text: Which sport uses a bat and a ball and is loved in India?
questions.explanation: Cricket uses a bat and ball and is very popular in India.

questions.text: ____ Gandhi is called the Father of the Nation.
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi is called the Father of the Nation.

questions.text: Hockey is the game where players use a stick and a ball.
questions.explanation: In hockey players use a stick to hit the ball. True.

questions.text: Which animal is known as 'man's best friend'?
questions.explanation: The dog is called man's best friend because it is loyal.
